theme_and_archetype.rationale: To achieve a venture-backed cybersecurity startup aesthetic (CrowdStrike, SentinelOne), we combine the authority and precision of Archetype 4 with the modern, immersive data-viz language of dark mode dashboards. The UI must feel like a high-end threat intelligence platform rather than a generic marketing site.

headings.usage: H1-H4. Used for high-impact section titles and hero messaging to provide a modern, geometric tech startup feel.

body.usage: Paragraphs, data tables, labels, and standard UI text. Optimized for legibility in dense data layouts.

monospace.usage: Risk scores, IP addresses, threat intel data points, and code snippets to reinforce the 'cybersecurity command center' aesthetic.

background.description: Deep Space Blue/Black - Core application background

surface.description: Slate 900 for cards, slightly lighter for elevated modals. Glass uses Slate 900 with opacity.

primary.description: Indigo 600 - Main call to action, active states, progress indicators.

secondary.description: Cyan 500 - Data visualization accents, secondary buttons, interactive highlights.

layout_and_spacing.optical_alignment: Align text left for readability in data-heavy contexts. Use center alignment strictly for Hero headers and Section headers (H2) if they span full width.

surfaces.tracing_beam_card: relative overflow-hidden rounded-2xl bg-[#0F172A] border border-white/10 before:absolute before:inset-0 before:bg-gradient-to-r before:from-indigo-500/0 before:via-indigo-500/20 before:to-indigo-500/0 before:translate-x-[-100%] hover:before:animate-[shimmer_2s_infinite]
surfaces.nav_header: fixed top-0 w-full z-50 backdrop-blur-xl bg-[#050816]/80 border-b border-white/5

buttons.primary: bg-indigo-600 hover:bg-indigo-700 text-white font-semibold py-3 px-6 rounded-lg transition-all shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(79,70,229,0.3)] hover:shadow-[0_0_30px_rgba(79,70,229,0.5)]
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent border border-white/20 hover:border-cyan-500 hover:text-cyan-400 text-white font-medium py-3 px-6 rounded-lg transition-all

components.inputs: bg-[#0F172A] border border-white/10 text-white rounded-lg focus:border-indigo-500 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-indigo-500 transition-all
components.badges: inline-flex items-center px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-indigo-500/10 text-indigo-400 border border-indigo-500/20
components.data_viz: Use Recharts. Use secondary (#06B6D4) and primary (#4F46E5) for lines/bars. Implement tooltips with Glassmorphism surface.

motion.page_transitions: Fade in with slight upward translation on mount (Framer Motion: { opacity: 0, y: 20 } to { opacity: 1, y: 0 })
motion.staggered_lists: Stagger children by 0.1s for feature grids and threat cards.

hero_backgrounds.description: Abstract technology / network node background for Hero Section (Requires heavy dark overlay to ensure text contrast).
Blue data lines, excellent for 'How it Works' or 'Platform Overview' background.

cyber_visuals.description: Matrix/Terminal code for Threat Landscape section headers or abstract feature representations.
Terminal prompt for 'Solutions for Developers/Security Teams' cards.

office_team.description: Dark office for 'About Us' or 'Enterprise Solutions' backdrop.

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a venture-backed cybersecurity marketing/SaaS hybrid site. Dark mode is strictly enforced using #050816 as the base background.
HTML structure MUST be used for marketing grid layouts (Bento grids). Use Shadcn components strictly for forms, inputs, and interactive data elements.
Lucide React or Phosphor Icons are mandatory for threat categories (Shield, Eye, AlertTriangle, Fingerprint).
Every hero or heavy image background MUST have a dark overlay (e.g., bg-black/60) to ensure the white/slate text meets APCA/WCAG contrast ratios.
Implement Lucide icons with custom sizes to act as 'threat badges'.
Mock up the 'Platform Dashboard' section using real Recharts line/area charts and metric cards (using Inter monospace fonts for numbers) to make it look like a functioning SaaS.

CRITICAL: ALL interactive elements (buttons, links, form inputs) and key informational metrics (risk scores, threat alerts) MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute in kebab-case representing their role. Example: data-testid='hero-cta-button', data-testid='risk-score-value'.
